baseline.input_text: This benchmark checks whether the audio model can generate short responses quickly while preserving pronunciation, rhythm, and clarity.
baseline.label: baseline

metrics.hypothesis_normalized: this benchmark checks whether the audio model can generate short responses quickly while preserving pronunciation rhythm and clarity
metrics.reference_normalized: this benchmark checks whether the audio model can generate short responses quickly while preserving pronunciation rhythm and clarity

baseline.input_text: A realtime voice agent needs low latency, consistent audio quality, accurate pronunciation, and predictable performance across short and medium length replies during normal conversation.
baseline.label: baseline

metrics.hypothesis_normalized: a real time voice agent needs low latency consistent audio quality accurate pronunciation and predictable performance across short and medium length replies during no normal conversation
metrics.reference_normalized: a realtime voice agent needs low latency consistent audio quality accurate pronunciation and predictable performance across short and medium length replies during normal conversation

baseline.transcript: A real-time voice agent needs low latency, consistent audio quality, accurate pronunciation, and predictable performance across short and medium length replies during no normal conversation.

baseline.input_text: This test measures whether the text to speech system can generate a useful spoken response fast enough for an interactive voice agent. The output should sound natural, remain intelligible, avoid skipped words, and maintain stable pacing while the benchmark records latency, throughput, memory usage, and transcription accuracy.
baseline.label: baseline

metrics.hypothesis_normalized: this test measures whether the text to speech system can generate a useful spoken response fast enough for an interactive voice agent the output should sound natural remain intelligible avoid skipped words and maintain stable pacing while the benchmark records latency throughput memory usage and transcription accuracy
metrics.reference_normalized: this test measures whether the text to speech system can generate a useful spoken response fast enough for an interactive voice agent the output should sound natural remain intelligible avoid skipped words and maintain stable pacing while the benchmark records latency throughput memory usage and transcription accuracy

baseline.transcript: This test measures whether the text-to-speech system can generate a useful spoken response fast enough for an interactive voice agent. The output should sound natural, remain intelligible, avoid skipped words, and maintain stable pacing while the benchmark records latency, throughput, memory usage, and transcription accuracy.

baseline.input_text: This longer benchmark checks whether the optimized generation path remains stable when the assistant produces a complete spoken answer. The system should keep the model loaded, avoid unnecessary transfers between CPU and GPU, reduce repeated preprocessing work, and generate audio faster than the original implementation without damaging intelligibility. The output should preserve the meaning of the prompt, pronounce the words clearly, avoid hallucinated phrases, avoid missing clauses, and maintain a natural speaking rhythm. The final report must compare baseline and optimized generation times, realtime factors, memory usage, bottlenecks, code changes, and transcription accuracy measured with Parakeet.
baseline.label: baseline
